generatedSentence: Our protagonist, a wanderer who backpacked through forgotten territories, always held strong values. He'd often follow a solitary path, much like a stubborn donkey, his thoughts often muffled by the wind. He had majored in xenolinguistics, a skill useful even in a dilapidated space station where he was an unexpected guest. Some locals believed in an ancient space god, their faith unwavering. He settled into a wobbly chair near the desolate planetary border, an area rumored to cause a strange jaundice-like affliction. Whispers of a necromancer manipulating the glowing neons of the city center were dismissed as the ramblings of a grouchy, whiny old man. The only reliable transport was a hitchhiking rig, carrying leftover supplies and gaudy alien jewelry. He sought perfection in his solitary observations, sometimes feeling he had bluffed his way into this strange reality, a recurring vision like a lost dreamcatcher. He saw eagles that glided with a strange enrichment in their flight patterns. With a nimble leap, he avoided a rogue drone, remembering the chaos of a distant thanksgiving meal that felt as alien as this planet's alien kitchen tech. A moment of self-reflection in a shattered vanity mirror revealed the faint blue glow of an unknown nitrogen-based pathogen, threatening to augment his very being. He knew this ancient stronghold was the source, its walls growing thicker with each passing cycle. Reports of a synthetic typhoid outbreak caused by the planet's peculiar flora, rumored to be sentient koalas that bloomed like vibrant fireworks, had reached the nautical outposts. An alien antibody was the only hope, yet its creator was notoriously gawky and had developed an unplayable game of celestial chess. He knew his mission was to observe, to prevent a cosmic casualty that could afflict countless systems. The planet's warehouse district showed signs of strange energy pooling, indicating something untrustworthy was at play, an anomaly that made him want to upchuck. He braced for the unlimited dangers ahead. Suddenly, a series of invisible bonds began untying around his wrists, a psychic trap. He offered no objection as a robotic dingo appeared, seemingly guiding him from his self-imposed exile. The dingo led him past holographic newsstand projections and abandoned apartments, an almost comical scene. His senses were amped, detecting an abnormal energy width emanating from a notoriously infamous sector. He had an inkling of what awaited him, as if a long-lost memory rejoined his consciousness. The path ahead was paved with strange, crystalline basalt and ancient, forgotten volumes of knowledge. His own fingernails felt brittle from tension. A disembodied voice began a chilling dictation, revealing the enemy's plan to make entire populations follow like lemmings. This was his moment of achievement, or utter failure. He couldn't afford a gaffe at this critical threshold. The danger was like a rapidly spreading tinder fire. He felt utterly incapable of stopping it, but knew he had to try, roughly estimating his chances. He recalled old raffles for alien livestock, a bizarre contrast to the current grim reality. A hulking catfish-like alien, operating a heavy loader, blocked his path. He knew it was just the start of a long evening. Beyond the shattered dome, a beautiful nebula swirled, oblivious to the impending armageddon. The entire conflict felt like an artificial construct, masterminded by an unseen assassin. He learned that these events had unfolded mysteriously for eons, a cosmic game of cat and mouse. Yet, he saw a peculiar majesty in the struggle, marking his progress with glowing markers on a mental map. The very air seemed to be silvering with arcane energy. He uttered no formal salutations to the unseen foe, only a grim determination to prevent widespread genocide. The true culprits were rogue hackers, manipulating the galactic taxpayer funds for their dark agenda. A strange, pulsating udder-like device was their source of power. He faced a final obstacle – a reinforced door requiring a specific keycard. This victory had to be earned, not given. He found the keycard hidden beneath a discarded alien newspaper, an accursed relic of a bygone era. Anyways, as the transition to dawn began, a rainy mist settled, leaving a strange prickling sensation on his skin, a promise of new adventures.
panelSentences: Our protagonist, a wanderer who backpacked through forgotten territories, always held strong values. He'd often follow a solitary path, much like a stubborn donkey, his thoughts often muffled by the wind. He had majored in xenolinguistics, a skill useful even in a dilapidated space station where he was an unexpected guest. Some locals believed in an ancient space god, their faith unwavering. He settled into a wobbly chair near the desolate planetary border, an area rumored to cause a strange jaundice-like affliction. Whispers of a necromancer manipulating the glowing neons of the city center were dismissed as the ramblings of a grouchy, whiny old man. The only reliable transport was a hitchhiking rig, carrying leftover supplies and gaudy alien jewelry. He sought perfection in his solitary observations, sometimes feeling he had bluffed his way into this strange reality, a recurring vision like a lost dreamcatcher. He saw eagles that glided with a strange enrichment in their flight patterns.

With a nimble leap, he avoided a rogue drone, remembering the chaos of a distant thanksgiving meal that felt as alien as this planet's alien kitchen tech. A moment of self-reflection in a shattered vanity mirror revealed the faint blue glow of an unknown nitrogen-based pathogen, threatening to augment his very being. He knew this ancient stronghold was the source, its walls growing thicker with each passing cycle. Reports of a synthetic typhoid outbreak caused by the planet's peculiar flora, rumored to be sentient koalas that bloomed like vibrant fireworks, had reached the nautical outposts. An alien antibody was the only hope, yet its creator was notoriously gawky and had developed an unplayable game of celestial chess. He knew his mission was to observe, to prevent a cosmic casualty that could afflict countless systems. The planet's warehouse district showed signs of strange energy pooling, indicating something untrustworthy was at play, an anomaly that made him want to upchuck. He braced for the unlimited dangers ahead.

Suddenly, a series of invisible bonds began untying around his wrists, a psychic trap. He offered no objection as a robotic dingo appeared, seemingly guiding him from his self-imposed exile. The dingo led him past holographic newsstand projections and abandoned apartments, an almost comical scene. His senses were amped, detecting an abnormal energy width emanating from a notoriously infamous sector. He had an inkling of what awaited him, as if a long-lost memory rejoined his consciousness. The path ahead was paved with strange, crystalline basalt and ancient, forgotten volumes of knowledge. His own fingernails felt brittle from tension. A disembodied voice began a chilling dictation, revealing the enemy's plan to make entire populations follow like lemmings. This was his moment of achievement, or utter failure. He couldn't afford a gaffe at this critical threshold. The danger was like a rapidly spreading tinder fire. He felt utterly incapable of stopping it, but knew he had to try, roughly estimating his chances. He recalled old raffles for alien livestock, a bizarre contrast to the current grim reality. A hulking catfish-like alien, operating a heavy loader, blocked his path.

He knew it was just the start of a long evening. Beyond the shattered dome, a beautiful nebula swirled, oblivious to the impending armageddon. The entire conflict felt like an artificial construct, masterminded by an unseen assassin. He learned that these events had unfolded mysteriously for eons, a cosmic game of cat and mouse. Yet, he saw a peculiar majesty in the struggle, marking his progress with glowing markers on a mental map. The very air seemed to be silvering with arcane energy. He uttered no formal salutations to the unseen foe, only a grim determination to prevent widespread genocide. The true culprits were rogue hackers, manipulating the galactic taxpayer funds for their dark agenda. A strange, pulsating udder-like device was their source of power. He faced a final obstacle – a reinforced door requiring a specific keycard. This victory had to be earned, not given. He found the keycard hidden beneath a discarded alien newspaper, an accursed relic of a bygone era. Anyways, as the transition to dawn began, a rainy mist settled, leaving a strange prickling sensation on his skin, a promise of new adventures.
rawPanelSentences: Our protagonist, a wanderer who **backpacked** through forgotten territories, always held strong **values**. He'd often **follow** a solitary path, much like a stubborn **donkey**, his thoughts often **muffled** by the wind. He had **majored** in xenolinguistics, a skill useful even in a dilapidated space **station** where he was an unexpected **guest**. Some locals believed in an ancient space **god**, their faith unwavering. He settled into a wobbly **chair** near the desolate planetary **border**, an area rumored to cause a strange **jaundice**-like affliction. Whispers of a **necromancer** manipulating the glowing **neons** of the city center were dismissed as the ramblings of a **grouchy**, **whiny** old man. The only reliable **transport** was a **hitchhiking** rig, carrying **leftover** supplies and gaudy alien **jewelry**. He sought **perfection** in his solitary observations, sometimes feeling he had **bluffed** his way into this strange reality, a recurring vision like a lost **dreamcatcher**. He saw **eagles** that glided with a strange **enrichment** in their flight patterns.

With a **nimble** leap, he avoided a rogue drone, remembering the chaos of a distant **thanksgiving** meal that felt as alien as this planet's alien **kitchen** tech. A moment of self-reflection in a shattered **vanity** mirror revealed the faint blue glow of an unknown **nitrogen**-based pathogen, threatening to **augment** his very being. He knew this ancient **stronghold** was the source, its walls growing **thicker** with each passing cycle. Reports of a synthetic **typhoid** outbreak caused by the planet's peculiar flora, rumored to be sentient **koalas** that bloomed like vibrant **fireworks**, had reached the **nautical** outposts. An alien **antibody** was the only hope, yet its creator was notoriously **gawky** and had developed an **unplayable** game of celestial chess. He knew his mission was to **observe**, to prevent a cosmic **casualty** that could **afflict** countless systems. The planet's **warehouse** district showed signs of strange energy **pooling**, indicating something **untrustworthy** was at play, an anomaly that made him want to **upchuck**. He braced for the **unlimited** dangers ahead.

Suddenly, a series of invisible bonds began **untying** around his wrists, a psychic trap. He offered no **objection** as a robotic **dingo** appeared, seemingly guiding him from his self-imposed **exile**. The dingo led him past holographic **newsstand** projections and abandoned **apartments**, an almost comical scene. His senses were **amped**, detecting an abnormal energy **width** emanating from a notoriously **infamous** sector. He had an **inkling** of what awaited him, as if a long-lost memory **rejoined** his consciousness. The path ahead was paved with strange, crystalline **basalt** and ancient, forgotten **volumes** of knowledge. His own **fingernails** felt brittle from tension. A disembodied voice began a chilling **dictation**, revealing the enemy's plan to make entire populations follow like **lemmings**. This was his moment of **achievement**, or utter failure. He couldn't afford a **gaffe** at this critical **threshold**. The danger was like a rapidly spreading **tinder** fire. He felt utterly **incapable** of stopping it, but knew he had to try, **roughly** estimating his chances. He recalled old **raffles** for alien livestock, a bizarre contrast to the current grim reality. A hulking **catfish**-like alien, operating a heavy **loader**, blocked his path.

He knew it was **just** the start of a long **evening**. Beyond the shattered dome, a beautiful **nebula** swirled, oblivious to the impending **armageddon**. The entire conflict felt like an **artificial** construct, masterminded by an unseen **assassin**. He learned that these events had unfolded **mysteriously** for eons, a cosmic game of cat and **mouse**. Yet, he saw a peculiar **majesty** in the struggle, marking his progress with glowing **markers** on a mental map. The very air seemed to be **silvering** with arcane energy. He uttered no formal **salutations** to the unseen foe, only a grim determination to prevent widespread **genocide**. The true culprits were rogue **hackers**, manipulating the galactic **taxpayer** funds for their dark agenda. A strange, pulsating **udder**-like device was their source of power. He faced a final **obstacle** – a reinforced door requiring a specific **keycard**. This victory had to be **earned**, not given. He found the keycard hidden beneath a discarded alien **newspaper**, an **accursed** relic of a bygone era. **Anyways**, as the **transition** to dawn began, a **rainy** mist settled, leaving a strange **prickling** sensation on his skin, a promise of new adventures.
imagePrompt: A professional 4-panel comic book page, 2x2 grid, sequential art, vibrant neon futurism, thick white gutters, clean black borders. A cloaked figure with glowing green eyes and a cybernetic arm. TOP-LEFT PANEL: A cloaked figure with glowing green eyes and a cybernetic arm stands at a desolate planetary border, observing strange neon structures in the distance, full body, wide shot. TOP-RIGHT PANEL: The cloaked figure, medium shot, reacts with focused intensity as holographic dingoes emerge from a chaotic, brightly lit warehouse entrance. BOTTOM-LEFT PANEL: A dynamic low angle shot of the cloaked figure, cybernetic arm extended, deflecting energy pulses from an unseen threat, amidst a shower of basalt shards. BOTTOM-RIGHT PANEL: A wide shot of the cloaked figure, victorious, silhouetted against a dramatic, swirling nebula, holding a glowing keycard, with misty rain falling.

definitions.definition: To hike and camp overnight in backcountry with one's gear carried in a backpack
To engage in low-cost, generally urban, travel with minimal luggage and frugal accommodations
To place or carry (an item or items) in a backpack
The quality (positive or negative) that renders something desirable or valuable.
definitions.example: The Shakespearean Shylock is of dubious value in the modern world.

definitions.definition: The degree of importance given to something.
definitions.example: The value of my children's happiness is second only to that of my wife.

definitions.definition: That which is valued or highly esteemed, such as one's morals, morality, or belief system.
definitions.example: He does not share his parents' values.

definitions.definition: The amount (of money or goods or services) that is considered to be a fair equivalent for something else.
The relative duration of a musical note.
definitions.example: The value of a crotchet is twice that of a quaver.

definitions.definition: The relative darkness or lightness of a color in (a specific area of) a painting etc.
Any definite numerical quantity or other mathematical object, determined by being measured, computed, or otherwise defined.
definitions.example: The exact value of pi cannot be represented in decimal notation.

definitions.definition: Precise meaning; import.
definitions.example: the value of a word; the value of a legal instrument

definitions.definition: (in the plural) The valuable ingredients to be obtained by treating a mass or compound; specifically, the precious metals contained in rock, gravel, etc.
definitions.example: The vein carries good values.

definitions.definition: Esteem; regard.
Valour; also spelled valew.
To estimate the value of; judge the worth of something.
definitions.example: I will have the family jewels valued by a professional.

definitions.definition: To fix or determine the value of; assign a value to, as of jewelry or art work.
To regard highly; think much of; place importance upon.
definitions.example: Gold was valued highly among the Romans.

definitions.definition: To hold dear.
definitions.example: I value these old photographs.

definitions.definition: (sometimes attributive) In billiards and similar games, a stroke causing a ball to follow another ball after hitting it.
definitions.example: a follow shot

definitions.definition: The act of following another user's online activity.
To go after; to pursue; to move behind in the same path or direction.
definitions.example: Follow that car!

definitions.definition: To go or come after in a sequence.
definitions.example: B follows A in the alphabet.

definitions.definition: To carry out (orders, instructions, etc.).
definitions.example: Follow these instructions to the letter.

definitions.definition: To live one's life according to (religion, teachings, etc).
To understand, to pay attention to.
definitions.example: Do you follow me?

definitions.definition: To watch, to keep track of (reports of) some event or person.
definitions.example: I followed the incumbent throughout the election.

definitions.definition: To subscribe to see content from an account on a social media platform.
definitions.example: If you want to see more of our articles, follow us on Twitter.

definitions.definition: To be a logical consequence of something.
definitions.example: If you don't practise proper hygiene, illness is sure to follow.

definitions.definition: To walk in, as a road or course; to attend upon closely, as a profession or calling.
A domestic animal, Equus asinus asinus, similar to a horse
A stubborn person
A fool
A small auxiliary engine
A bad poker player
To wrap (a person, face etc.) in fabric or another covering, for warmth or protection; often with up.
To wrap up or cover (a source of noise) in order to deaden the sound.
definitions.example: to muffle the strings of a drum, or that part of an oar which rests in the rowlock

definitions.definition: To mute or deaden (a sound etc.).
To speak indistinctly, or without clear articulation.
To prevent seeing, or hearing, or speaking, by wraps bound about the head; to blindfold; to deafen.
(of a sound) Stifled or covered up; diminished by interference.
To concentrate on a particular area of study as a student in a college or university
definitions.example: I have decided to major in mathematics.

definitions.definition: A stopping place.
A place where workers are stationed.
Any of the Stations of the Cross.
The Roman Catholic fast of the fourth and sixth days of the week, Wednesday and Friday, in memory of the council which condemned Christ, and of his passion.
A church in which the procession of the clergy halts on stated days to say stated prayers.
Standing; rank; position.
definitions.example: She had ambitions beyond her station.

definitions.definition: A broadcasting entity.
definitions.example: I used to listen to that radio station.

definitions.definition: A harbour or cove with a foreshore suitable for a facility to support nearby fishing.
Any of a sequence of equally spaced points along a path.
The particular place, or kind of situation, in which a species naturally occurs; a habitat.
An enlargement in a shaft or galley, used as a landing, or passing place, or for the accommodation of a pump, tank, etc.
Post assigned; office; the part or department of public duty which a person is appointed to perform; sphere of duty or occupation; employment.
The position of the foetal head in relation to the distance from the ischial spines, measured in centimetres.
The fact of standing still; motionlessness, stasis.
The apparent standing still of a superior planet just before it begins or ends its retrograde motion.
(usually passive) To put in place to perform a task.
definitions.example: I was stationed on the pier.

definitions.definition: To put in place to perform military duty.
definitions.example: I was stationed at Fort Richie.

definitions.definition: A recipient of hospitality, specifically someone staying by invitation at the house of another.
definitions.example: The guests were let in by the butler.

definitions.definition: A patron or customer in a hotel etc.
definitions.example: Guests must vacate their rooms by 10 o'clock on their day of departure.

definitions.definition: An invited visitor or performer to an institution or to a broadcast.
definitions.example: The guest for the broadcast was a leading footballer.

definitions.definition: A user given temporary access to a system despite not having an account of their own.
Any insect that lives in the nest of another without compulsion and usually not as a parasite.
An inquiline.
To appear as a guest, especially on a broadcast
As a musician, to play as a guest, providing an instrument that a band/orchestra does not normally have in its line up (for instance, percussion in a string band)
To receive or entertain hospitably.
A being such as a monotheistic God: a single divine creator and ruler of the universe.
The single deity of various monotheistic religions, especially the deity of Judaism, Christianity, and Islam.
definitions.example: Dawn believes in God, but Willow believes in multiple gods and goddesses.

definitions.definition: The single male deity of various bitheistic or duotheistic religions.
A deity or supreme being; a supernatural, typically immortal, being with superior powers, to which personhood is attributed.
definitions.example: The most frequently used name for the Islamic god is Allah.

definitions.definition: An idol.
definitions.example: Leo Messi is my god!

definitions.definition: A person in a high position of authority, importance or influence.
A powerful ruler or tyrant.
An exceedingly handsome man.
definitions.example: Lounging on the beach were several Greek gods.

definitions.definition: The person who owns and runs a multi-user dungeon.
To idolize.
To deify.
An item of furniture used to sit on or in, comprising a seat, legs, back, and sometimes arm rests, for use by one person. Compare stool, couch, sofa, settee, loveseat and bench.
definitions.example: All I need to weather a snowstorm is hot coffee, a warm fire, a good book and a comfortable chair.

definitions.definition: The seating position of a particular musician in an orchestra.
definitions.example: My violin teacher used to play first chair with the Boston Pops.

definitions.definition: An iron block used on railways to support the rails and secure them to the sleepers, and similar devices.
One of two possible conformers of cyclohexane rings (the other being boat), shaped roughly like a chair.
A distinguished professorship at a university.
A vehicle for one person; either a sedan borne upon poles, or a two-wheeled carriage drawn by one horse; a gig.
The seat or office of a person in authority, such as a judge or bishop.
To act as chairperson at; to preside over.
definitions.example: Bob will chair tomorrow's meeting.

definitions.definition: To carry in a seated position upon one's shoulders, especially in celebration or victory.
To award a chair to (a winning poet) at a Welsh eisteddfod.
definitions.example: The poet was chaired at the national Eisteddfod.

definitions.definition: A chairman or chairwoman, someone who presides over a meeting, board, etc.
A chair-like device used for performing execution by electrocution.
An electrically powered wheelchair.
A transitional hold in which an attacking wrestler hoists an opponent up onto his/her shoulders so that they are both facing in the same direction.
The outer edge of something.
definitions.example: the borders of the garden

definitions.definition: A decorative strip around the edge of something.
definitions.example: There's a nice frilly border around the picture frame.

definitions.definition: A strip of ground in which ornamental plants are grown.
The line or frontier area separating political or geographical regions.
definitions.example: The border between Canada and USA is the longest in the world.

definitions.definition: Border morris or border dancing; a vigorous style of traditional English dance originating from villages along the border between England and Wales, performed by a team of dancers usually with their faces disguised with black makeup.
A string that is both a prefix and a suffix of another particular string.
To put a border on something.
To form a border around; to bound.
To lie on, or adjacent to, a border of.
definitions.example: Denmark borders Germany to the south.

definitions.definition: To touch at a border (with on, upon, or with).
definitions.example: Connecticut borders on Massachusetts.

definitions.definition: To approach; to come near to; to verge (with on or upon).
A morbid condition, characterized by yellowness of the eyes, skin, and urine.
A feeling of bitterness, resentment or jealousy.
To affect with jaundice; to color by prejudice or envy; to prejudice.
A person who practices or performs necromancy.
The chemical element (symbol Ne) with an atomic number of 10. The lightest of the noble gases, it is a colourless, odorless inert gas.
A form or sample of the element.
Neon signs or lights, collectively.
A neon tetra fish.
(originally student slang) Irritable; easily upset; angry; tending to complain.
definitions.example: His boss gets grouchy when deadlines draw near.

definitions.definition: Whining; tending to whine or complain.
definitions.example: Emily is very whiny; she keeps complaining about trivial things.

definitions.definition: An act of transporting; conveyance.
definitions.example: The transport of goods is not included in the price given on the website.

definitions.definition: The state of being transported by emotion; rapture.
A vehicle used to transport (passengers, mail, freight, troops etc.)
A tractor-trailer.
The system of transporting passengers, etc. in a particular region; the vehicles used in such a system.
definitions.example: The local transport received a big boost as part of the mayor's infrastructural plans.

definitions.definition: A device that moves recording tape across the read/write heads of a tape recorder or video recorder etc.
A deported convict.
To carry or bear from one place to another; to remove; to convey.
definitions.example: to transport goods; to transport troops

definitions.definition: To deport to a penal colony.
To move (someone) to strong emotion; to carry away.
definitions.example: Music transports the soul.

definitions.definition: The quality or state of being perfect or complete, so that nothing substandard remains; the highest attainable state or degree of excellence
definitions.example: The system runs to perfection.

definitions.definition: A quality, endowment, or acquirement completely excellent; an ideal; faultlessness; especially, the divine attribute of complete excellence.
To perfect.
To make a bluff; to give the impression that one's hand is stronger than it is.
definitions.example: John bluffed by betting without even a pair.

definitions.definition: (by analogy) To frighten or deter with a false show of strength or confidence; to give a false impression of strength or temerity in order to intimidate and gain some advantage.
definitions.example: The government claims it will call an election if this bill does not pass. Is it truly ready to do so, or is it bluffing?

definitions.definition: To take advantage by bluffing.
definitions.example: We bluffed our way past the guards.

definitions.definition: To fluff, puff or swell up.
A decorative Native American object in the form of a hoop and net with attachments such as feathers, traditionally believed by the Ojibwa to “filter out” bad dreams.
To try to get a ride in a passing vehicle while standing at the side of a road, generally by either sticking out one's finger or thumb or holding a sign with one's stated destination.

To be carried along with something else, for example Genetic Hitchhiking where a gene is propagated because it occurs in conjunction with a favourable mutation, or Cultural Hitchhiking where a cultural trait spreads with a technologically advanced population.
Something left behind; an excess or remainder.
definitions.example: It's a leftover from yesterday, but it's still perfectly good.

definitions.definition: (chiefly in the plural, usually of food) Remaining after a meal is complete or eaten for a later meal or snack.
definitions.example: Not leftovers again!

definitions.definition: Remaining; left behind; extra; in reserve.
definitions.example: Do you want some of the leftover supplies from the event?

definitions.definition: Collectively, personal ornamentation such as rings, necklaces, brooches and bracelets, made of precious metals and sometimes set with gemstones.
definitions.example: She had more jewellery ornamented about her than any three ladies needed.

definitions.definition: The act of enriching or something enriched.
The process of making enriched uranium.
The addition of sugar to grape juice used to make wine; chaptalization.
Extracurricular activities for students.
Any of several large carnivorous and carrion-eating birds in the family Accipitridae, having a powerful hooked bill and keen vision.
A gold coin with a face value of ten dollars, formerly used in the United States.
A 13th-century coin minted in Europe and circulated in England as a debased sterling silver penny, outlawed under Edward I.
A score of two under par for a hole.
To score an eagle.
Adept at taking or grasping
definitions.example: nimble fingers

definitions.definition: Quick and light in movement or action.
definitions.example: He was too nimble for the assailant and easily escaped his grasp.

definitions.definition: Quick-witted and alert.
definitions.example: She has a nimble mind and can improvise in any situation.

definitions.definition: An expression of gratitude.
A short prayer said at meals; grace, a benediction.
A public celebration in acknowledgement of divine favour.
A room or area for preparing food.
Cuisine.
The nape of a person's hairline, often referring to its uncombed or "nappy" look.
The percussion section of an orchestra.
A utensil for roasting meat.
A domesticated or uneducated form of a language.
A public gaming room in a casino.
Anything eaten as a relish with bread, potatoes, etc.
To do kitchen work; to prepare food.
To embellish a basic food; to season, add condiments, etc.
(by extension) To embellish; to dress up.
That which is vain, futile, or worthless; that which is of no value, use or profit.
Excessive pride in or admiration of one's own abilities, appearance or achievements.

A dressing table used to apply makeup, preen, and coif hair. The table is normally quite low and similar to a desk, with drawers and one or more mirrors on top. Either a chair or bench is used to sit upon.
A washbasin installed into a permanently fixed storage unit, used as an item of bathroom furniture.
Emptiness.
Any idea, theory or statement that is without foundation.
definitions.example: It is a vanity to say that if two stones are dropped from a tower, the heavier will experience the greater acceleration.

definitions.definition: The chemical element (symbol N) with an atomic number of 7 and atomic weight of 14.0067. It is a colorless and odorless gas.
Molecular nitrogen (N2), a colorless, odorless gas at room temperature.
A specific nitrogen within a chemical formula, or a specific isotope of nitrogen
definitions.example: The two nitrogens are located next to one another on the ring.

definitions.definition: (grammar) In some Indo-European languages, a prefix e- (a- in Sanskrit) indicating a past tense of a verb.
(grammar) In some Bantu languages, an additional vowel prepended to the noun prefix.
An increase.
To increase; to make larger or supplement.
definitions.example: The money from renting out a spare room can augment a salary.

definitions.definition: To grow; to increase; to become greater.
To slow the tempo or meter, e.g. for a dramatic or stately passage.
To increase an interval, especially the largest interval in a triad, by a half step (chromatic semitone).
(grammar) To add an augment to.
A place built to withstand attack; a fortress.
A place of domination by, or refuge or survival of, a particular group or idea.
definitions.example: The last stronghold of the Cornish language.

definitions.definition: Relatively great in extent from one surface to the opposite in its smallest solid dimension.
Measuring a certain number of units in this dimension.
definitions.example: I want some planks that are two inches thick.

definitions.definition: Heavy in build; thickset.
definitions.example: He had such a thick neck that he had to turn his body to look to the side.

definitions.definition: Densely crowded or packed.
definitions.example: We walked through thick undergrowth.

definitions.definition: Having a viscous consistency.
definitions.example: My mum’s gravy was thick but at least it moved about.

definitions.definition: Abounding in number.
definitions.example: The room was thick with reporters.

definitions.definition: Impenetrable to sight.
definitions.example: We drove through thick fog.

definitions.definition: (Of an accent) Prominent, strong.
Stupid.
definitions.example: He was as thick as two short planks.

definitions.definition: Friendly or intimate.
definitions.example: They were as thick as thieves.

definitions.definition: Deep, intense, or profound.

definitions.definition: Troublesome; unreasonable
(chiefly of women) Curvy and voluptuous, and especially having large hips.
In a thick manner.

definitions.definition: Frequently or numerously.
definitions.example: The arrows flew thick and fast around us.

definitions.definition: Typhoid fever
To greet in French with "bonjour".
(in French contexts) good morning
A tree-dwelling marsupial, Phascolarctos cinereus, that resembles a small bear with a broad head, large ears and sharp claws, mainly found in eastern Australia.

A device using gunpowder and other chemicals which, when lit, emits a combination of coloured flames, sparks, whistles or bangs, and sometimes made to rocket high into the sky before exploding, used for entertainment or celebration.
definitions.example: Commercial firework displays are commonly launched from boats or other vessels

definitions.definition: An event or a display where fireworks are set off.
A boisterous or violent event or situation.
definitions.example: I left the room after John came home drunk but before the fireworks went off.

definitions.definition: Relating to or involving ships or shipping or navigation or seamen.
definitions.example: nautical charts

definitions.definition: A protein produced by B-lymphocytes that binds to a specific antigen.
An awkward, ungainly person.
Awkward, ungainly; lacking grace or dexterity in movement
(of an audio or visual recording) Unable to be played on specified equipment, or at all.
(of the delivery of a ball) Impossible to play or to defend against.
That cannot be played, or is so tedious, complicated, buggy, etc. as to discourage or preclude playing.
Not having religious faith.
Not keeping good faith; disloyal; not faithful.
Adulterous.
Not honest or upright.
Negligent or imperfect.

definitions.definition: Not faithfully rendering the meaning of the source language; incorrect.
To notice or view, especially carefully or with attention to detail.
definitions.example: From this vantage point we can observe the behavior of the animals in their natural habitat.

definitions.definition: To follow or obey the custom, practice, or rules (especially of a religion).
definitions.example: Please observe all posted speed limits.

definitions.definition: To take note of and celebrate (a holiday or similar occurrence).
To comment on something; to make an observation.
definitions.example: The senator observed that the bill would be detrimental to his constituents.

definitions.definition: Something that happens by chance, especially an unfortunate event; an accident, a disaster.
A person suffering from injuries or who has been killed due to an accident or through an act of violence.
Specifically, a person who has been killed (not only injured) due to an accident or through an act of violence; a fatality.
A person in military service who becomes unavailable for duty, for any reason (notably death, injury, illness, capture, or desertion).
The accident and emergency department of a hospital.
An incidental charge or payment.
Chance nature; randomness.
To cause (someone) pain, suffering or distress.
To strike or cast down; to overthrow.
To make low or humble.
A place for storing large amounts of products. In logistics, a place where products go to from the manufacturer before going to the retailer.
To store in a warehouse or similar.
To confine (a person) to an institution for a long period.
To acquire and then shelve, simply to prevent competitors from acquiring it.
definitions.example: the warehousing of syndicated TV shows

definitions.definition: (of a liquid) To form a pool.
To put together; contribute to a common fund, on the basis of a mutual division of profits or losses; to make a common interest of.
definitions.example: We must pool our resources.

definitions.definition: To combine or contribute with others, as for a commercial, speculative, or gambling transaction.
(resource management) Grouping together of various resources or assets.
Not deserving of trust; unreliable.
Vomit.
To vomit.
definitions.example: Joel began to upchuck all over the grandstand.

definitions.definition: Limitless or without bounds; unrestricted
To loosen, as something interlaced or knotted; to disengage the parts of.
definitions.example: to untie a knot

definitions.definition: To free from fastening or from restraint; to let loose; to unbind.
To resolve; to unfold; to clear.
To become untied or loosed.
In the Perl programming language, to undo the process of tying, so that a variable uses default instead of custom functionality.
The act by which something is untied.
The act of objecting.
A statement expressing opposition, or a reason or cause for expressing opposition (generally followed by the adposition to).
definitions.example: I have no objection to any person's religion.

definitions.definition: An official protest raised in a court of law during a legal trial over a violation of the rules of the court by the opposing party.
Canis lupus dingo, a wild dog native to Australia.
The state of being banished from one's home or country.

definitions.definition: Someone who is banished from their home or country.
definitions.example: She lived as an exile.

definitions.definition: To send into exile.
An open stall, often on a street, where newspapers and magazines are on sale to the public
A complete domicile occupying only part of a building, especially one for rent; a flat.
A suite of rooms within a domicile, designated for a specific person or persons and including a bedroom.
A division of an enclosure that is separate from others; a compartment
(COM) A conceptual space used for separation in the threading architecture. Objects in one apartment cannot directly access those in another, but must use a proxy.
(usually with up) To amplify.

definitions.definition: (usually with up) To excite.
(usually with up) To intensify or increase.
definitions.example: 2014, Andy Paul, Amp Up Your Sales, AMACOM Books

definitions.definition: The state of being wide.
The measurement of the extent of something from side to side.
A piece of material measured along its smaller dimension, especially fabric.
The horizontal distance between a batsman and the ball as it passes him.
The use of all the width of the pitch, from one side to the other.

definitions.definition: Having a bad reputation, disreputable; notoriously bad, unpleasant or evil; widely known, especially for something bad.
definitions.example: He was an infamous perjurer.

definitions.definition: Causing infamy; disgraceful.
definitions.example: This infamous deed tarnishes all involved.

definitions.definition: Subject to a judicial punishment that deprived the infamous person of certain rights; this included a prohibition against holding public office, exercising the franchise, receiving a public pension, serving on a jury, or giving testimony in a court of law.
Usually preceded by forms of to give: a slight hint, implication, or suggestion given.
Often preceded by forms of to get or to have: an imprecise idea or slight knowledge of something; a suspicion.
A desire, an inclination.
To join again; to unite after separation.
To come, or go, again into the presence of; to join the company of again.
To state in reply; -- followed by an object clause.
: To answer to a reply.
To answer, as the defendant to the plaintiff's replication.

In US patent law To re-insert a patent claim, typically after allowance of a patent application, applied to patent claims that had been withdrawn from examination under a restriction requirement, based on rejoinder .
A hard mafic igneous rock of varied mineral content; volcanic in origin, which makes up much of the Earth's oceanic crust.
A type of unglazed pottery.
A three-dimensional measure of space that comprises a length, a width and a height. It is measured in units of cubic centimeters in metric, cubic inches or cubic feet in English measurement.
definitions.example: The room is 9x12x8, so its volume is 864 cubic feet.

definitions.definition: Strength of sound; loudness.
definitions.example: Please turn down the volume on the stereo.

definitions.definition: The issues of a periodical over a period of one year.
definitions.example: I looked at this week's copy of the magazine. It was volume 23, issue 45.

definitions.definition: A bound book.
A single book of a publication issued in multi-book format, such as an encyclopedia.

definitions.definition: A roll or scroll, which was the form of ancient books.
Quantity.
definitions.example: The volume of ticket sales decreased this week.

definitions.definition: A rounded mass or convolution.
The total supply of money in circulation or, less frequently, total amount of credit extended, within a specified national market or worldwide.
An accessible storage area with a single file system, typically resident on a single partition of a hard disk.
To be conveyed through the air, waft.
To cause to move through the air, waft.
To swell.
The hard, flat translucent covering near the tip of a human finger, useful for scratching and fine manipulation.
Dictating, the process of speaking for someone else to write down the words

definitions.definition: An activity in school where the teacher reads a passage aloud and the students write it down
definitions.example: 1908: Lucy Maud Montgomery, Anne of Green Gables - We had reading and geography and Canadian history and dictation today.

definitions.definition: The act of ordering or commanding
definitions.example: 1852: Lysander Spooner, An Essay on the Trial by Jury - ...jurors in England have formerly understood it to be their right and duty to judge only according to their consciences, and not to submit to any dictation from the court, either as to law or fact.

definitions.definition: Orders given in an overbearing manner
definitions.example: His habit, even with friends, was that of dictation.

definitions.definition: A small Arctic and Subarctic rodent from any of six genera of similar rodents.
Any member of a group given to conformity or groupthink, especially a group poised to follow a leader off a cliff.
The act of achieving or performing; a successful performance; accomplishment
A great or heroic deed or feat; something accomplished by valor or boldness
An escutcheon or ensign armorial; now generally applied to the funeral shield commonly called hatchment.
An award for completing a particular task or meeting an objective in a video game.

definitions.definition: (grammar) The lexical aspect (aktionsart) of verbs or predicates that change in an instant.
A foolish and embarrassing error, especially one made in public.
The bottom-most part of a doorway that one crosses to enter; a sill.
(by extension) An entrance; the door or gate of a house.
(by extension) Any end or boundary.
The outset of something; the point of entry, or the beginning of an action.
The start of the landing area of a runway.
The quantitative point at which an action is triggered, especially a lower limit.
The wage or salary at which income tax becomes due.
The point where one mentally or physically is vulnerable in response to provocation or to particular things in general. As in emotions, stress, or pain.
Small dry sticks and finely-divided fibrous matter etc., used to help light a fire.
To set fire to; torch.
One who is morally or mentally weak or inefficient; an imbecile; a simpleton.
Not capable (of doing something); unable.

definitions.definition: Not in a state to receive; not receptive; not susceptible; not able to admit.
definitions.example: incapable of pain, or pleasure; incapable of stain or injury

definitions.definition: In a rough manner; without kindness, softness, or gentleness.
Unevenly or irregularly.
Without precision or exactness; imprecise but close to in quantity or amount; approximately.
A drawing, often held as a fundraiser, in which tickets or chances are sold to win a prize.

definitions.definition: A game of dice in which the player who throws three of the same number wins all the stakes.
To award something by means of a raffle or random drawing, often used with off.

definitions.definition: To participate in a raffle.

definitions.definition: Any fish of the order Siluriformes, mainly found in fresh water, lacking scales, and having barbels like whiskers around the mouth
To fish for catfish

definitions.definition: Agent noun of load; a person or device that loads.

definitions.definition: A program that prepares other programs for execution.
A tractor with a scoop, for example: bucket loader, front-end loader, wheel loader, etc.
Factually right, correct; factual.

definitions.definition: Rationally right, correct.
Morally right; upright, righteous, equitable; fair.

definitions.definition: Proper, adequate.
Only, simply, merely.

definitions.definition: (sentence adverb) Used to reduce the force of an imperative; simply.
definitions.example: Just follow the directions on the box.

definitions.definition: Used to convey a less serious or formal tone
definitions.example: I just called to say "hi".

definitions.definition: Used to show humility.
definitions.example: Lord, we just want to thank You and praise Your Name.

definitions.definition: (degree) absolutely, positively

definitions.definition: Moments ago, recently.
definitions.example: They just left, but you may leave a message at the desk.

definitions.definition: By a narrow margin; closely; nearly.

definitions.definition: Exactly, precisely, perfectly.
definitions.example: He wants everything just right for the big day.

definitions.definition: Expressing dismay or discontent.
The time of the day between dusk and night, when it gets dark.
The time of the day between the approximate time of midwinter dusk and midnight (compare afternoon); the period after the end of regular office working hours.
A concluding time period; a point in time near the end of something; the beginning of the end of something.
definitions.example: It was the evening of the Roman Empire.

definitions.definition: A party or gathering held in the evening.
A cloud in outer space consisting of gas or dust (e.g. a cloud formed after a star explodes).
A white spot or slight opacity of the cornea.
A cloudy appearance in the urine
The scene of a decisive conflict on a great scale.
Any great and crucial conflict.
Man-made; of artifice.

definitions.definition: False, misleading.

definitions.definition: Unnatural.

Someone who intentionally kills a person, especially a professional who kills a public or political figure.
Any ruthless killer.
To assassinate.
In a mysterious manner
Any small rodent of the genus Mus.
A member of the many small rodent and marsupial species resembling such a rodent.
A quiet or shy person.
(plural mice or, rarely, mouses) An input device that is moved over a pad or other flat surface to produce a corresponding movement of a pointer on a graphical display.
Hematoma.
A turn or lashing of spun yarn or small stuff, or a metallic clasp or fastening, uniting the point and shank of a hook to prevent its unhooking or straightening out.
A familiar term of endearment.
A match used in firing guns or blasting.
A small model of (a fragment of) Zermelo-Fraenkel set theory with desirable properties (depending on the context).
A small cushion for a woman's hair.
To move cautiously or furtively, in the manner of a mouse (the rodent) (frequently used in the phrasal verb to mouse around).
To hunt or catch mice (the rodents), usually of cats.
To close the mouth of a hook by a careful binding of marline or wire.

definitions.definition: To navigate by means of a computer mouse.
To tear, as a cat devours a mouse.
The quality of being impressive and great.

definitions.definition: An object used to mark a location.
Someone or something that marks.
A real or virtual objective, something to be aimed for.
A felt-tipped pen.

definitions.definition: Marks of ink left by this type of pen.
definitions.example: Susie has gotten marker all over the walls, and it looks dreadful.

definitions.definition: A signed note of a debt to be paid.
A nonmonetary debt owed to someone, especially in return for a favor.
definitions.example: We may not be able to do this alone. Maybe it’s time to call in some of our markers.

definitions.definition: A device that fires a paintball.
A defending player who stays close to an opponent in order to mark them.
A player employed by a private club and available to compete against members.
A gene or DNA sequence with a known location on a chromosome that can be used to identify individuals or species.
A recognition given by a competition authority that a company is the first to approach it to reveal the existence of a cartel, as a prelude to a formal application for leniency for the company.
To mark or write on (something) using a marker
To acquire a silvery colour.
To cover with silver, or with a silvery metal.

To make hoary, or white, like silver.
The process of coating a surface with a thin layer of silver (or a similar metal), especially so as to form a mirror.
The silver used in this kind of coating.
A greeting, salute, or address; a hello.
The act of greeting.
Quickening; excitement.
A greeting.
The systematic killing of substantial numbers of people on the basis of their ethnicity, religion, political beliefs, social status, or other particularities.
definitions.example: A genocide will always be followed by the denial that it ever happened.

definitions.definition: (by extension) The systematic suppression of ideas on the basis of cultural or ethnic origin; culturicide.
The elimination of an entire class of monsters by the player.
To commit genocide (against); to eliminate (a group of people) completely.
One who is expert at programming and solving problems with a computer.
One who uses a computer to gain unauthorized access to data, or to carry out malicious attacks.
A computer security professional.
Something that hacks; a tool or device for hacking.
A fork-shaped tool used to harvest root vegetables.
Someone who hacks.
One who is inexperienced or unskilled at a particular activity, especially a sport such as golf or tennis.
definitions.example: a tennis hacker

definitions.definition: One who operates a taxicab
A person who is subject to, liable for, or pays tax as opposed to a nontaxpayer who is neither the subject nor the object of revenue laws.
All of the people, collectively, in a population who pay tax (especially used in the context of the government financing something using the tax revenue).
definitions.example: 1945: I got into the bus full of taxpayers who were giving some money to a taxpayer who had on his taxpayer's stomach a little box which allowed the other taxpayers to continue their taxpayers' journeys. I noticed in this bus a taxpayer with a long taxpayer's neck and whose taxpayer's head bore a taxpayer's felt hat encircled by a plait the like of which no taxpayer ever wore before. Suddenly the said taxpayer peremptorily addressed a nearby taxpayer, complaining bitterly that he was purposely treading on his taxpayer's toes every time other taxpayers got on or off the taxpayers' bus. Then the angry taxpayer went and sat down in a seat for taxpayers which another taxpayer had just vacated. — Raymond Queneau, 'from Polyptotes' in Exercises in Style, 1945 (Eng. 1958)

definitions.definition: An organ formed of the mammary glands of female quadruped mammals, particularly ruminants such as cattle, goats, sheep and deer.
(impolite) A woman's breast.
Something that impedes, stands in the way of, or holds up progress
definitions.example: A big obstacle to understanding the manual was that it had been poorly translated from the Japanese.

definitions.definition: A usually plastic card which stores a digital signature that is used to operate an electronic access control lock.
To gain (success, reward, recognition) through applied effort or work.
definitions.example: You can have the s'mores: you earned them, clearing the walkway of snow so well.

definitions.definition: To receive payment for work.
definitions.example: He earns seven million dollars a year as CEO.  My bank account is only earning one percent interest.

definitions.definition: To receive payment for work.
definitions.example: Now that you are earning, you can start paying me rent.

definitions.definition: To cause (someone) to receive payment or reward.
definitions.example: My CD earns me six percent!

definitions.definition: To achieve by being worthy of.
definitions.example: to earn a spot in the top 20

definitions.definition: To curdle, as milk.
To long; to yearn.
To grieve.
A publication, usually published daily or weekly and usually printed on cheap, low-quality paper, containing news and other articles.
A quantity of or one of the types of paper on which newspapers are printed.
To cover with newspaper.
definitions.example: She newspapered one end of the room before painting the bookcase.

definitions.definition: To engage in the business of journalism (usually used only in the gerund, newspapering)
definitions.example: He newspapered his way through the South on the sports beat, avoiding dry towns.

definitions.definition: To harass in newspaper articles.
definitions.example: He was newspapered out of public life.

definitions.definition: To devote to destruction; to imprecate misery or evil upon; to curse; to execrate; to anathematize.
(prenominal) Hateful; detestable, loathsome.
Doomed to destruction or misery; cursed; anathematized.
In any way.
Anyway, anyhow, in any case.
The process of change from one form, state, style or place to another.
A word or phrase connecting one part of a discourse to another.
A brief modulation; a passage connecting two themes.
A change of key.
A point mutation in which one base is replaced by another of the same class (purine or pyrimidine); compare transversion.
A change from defense to attack, or attack to defense.

Professional special education assistance for children or adults in the process of leaving one educational environment or support program for another to relatively more independent living.
A change between forward and backward motion without stopping.
The process or act of changing from one gender role to another, or of bringing one's outward appearance in line with one's internal gender identity.
To make a transition.
To bring through a transition; to change.

definitions.definition: To change from one gender role to another, or bring one's outward appearance in line with one's internal gender identity.
Pouring with rain; wet; showery
definitions.example: Due to the rainy weather, we decided not to play in the park.

definitions.definition: To feel a prickle.
To cause (someone) to feel a prickle; to prick.
A sensation that prickles.
definitions.example: The patient reported intermittent pricklings down his right-hand side.
